render_data.title: Number of images vs angle between two mirrors

mirror.readoutTarget: Find the angle that gives 3 images (≈ 60°)

image_prompt: SVG: line graph, Number of images (y, 0-8) vs Angle between the mirrors in degrees (x, 0-180); decreasing inverse-style curve through points (60,7), (90,5), (120,2), (180,1).

topic: Plane mirrors — images vs angle (bar chart)
image_prompt: SVG: bar chart, Number of images (y, 0-8) vs Angle between the mirrors in degrees (x categories 45, 60, 120, 180); bars of height 7, 5, 2, 1.

SVG: distance-time graph for sound echoes, Distance / m (y, 0-600 step 100) vs Time / s (x, 0.0-1.6 step 0.2); scatter points (0.2,100), (0.6,200), (0.8,300), (1.0,400), (1.6,500) with best-fit straight line through the origin (gradient ~330 m/s).
SVG: stylized hazy city skyline shrouded in smog, distant grey towers fading into a tan haze blanket, forested green hills and a temple cluster in the foreground; caption 'Smog over a city'.

SVG: ionization chamber diagram — dirty air with dust enters from the left through inflow arrows, two glowing positive ionizing electrodes emit electron streams that charge the dust, the now-charged dust drifts to four long horizontal 'collection' plates (marked − at the left end, + at the right end) and sticks to the positive ends; clean air exits via green arrows on the right.

render_data.title: Classify the missing sections of the electromagnetic spectrum

image_prompt: SVG: electromagnetic spectrum drawn as a horizontal frequency axis (increasing frequency to the right) with division ticks; printed labels Microwave, Visible (with a small dispersion prism band), Ultraviolet, Gamma ray; three dashed empty answer slots marked '?' where the student drags the labels Radio (far left), Infra-red (before Visible) and X-rays (after Ultraviolet); a draggable tray at the top holds the boxes X-rays, Infra-red, Radio.

SVG: stylized X-ray of a cat in side profile (skull, arched spine of vertebrae, curved rib cage, leg and tail bones over a faint soft-tissue silhouette on a dark blue-green background); a small bright capsule between the shoulder blades is circled in yellow and labelled 'RFID'.

SVG: RFID internal structure — fingertips pinching a tiny glass-capsule RFID (labelled 'RFID') on the left; on the right a grain of rice for scale and an enlarged cutaway of the capsule spanning a 11.5 millimetres dimension line, with internal components labelled Microchip, Radio transmitter and Coil of wire (copper windings on a ferrite core).

SVG: ray diagram of reflection at a plane mirror — an upright 'Object' on the left, a hatched vertical mirror, two incident rays striking the mirror and reflecting back, two horizontal dashed 'Normal' lines at the reflection points, equal angle-of-incidence/angle-of-reflection arcs, and a faint dashed 'virtual image' behind the mirror.

topic: Speed of sound via echo — scene
image_prompt: SVG: outdoor scene — two students clap two wooden blocks together (clap burst) on the left, dashed sound rays travel out to a wall on the right and reflect back; a dimensioned arrow labelled 'Distance = 110 m' spans the ground between them and the wall; 'Wall' and 'Diagram not to scale' labels.

SVG: clean ruled data table titled 'Distance travelled and time for echo results' with columns Distance (m) and Time (s); rows 400/1.18, 200/0.601, 300/0.88, 500/1.46 (values exactly as recorded in the notebook).

render_data.title: Mass on a spring — effect of mass on time period

image_prompt: SVG: oscillation apparatus — a stopwatch reading 00:00 on the left; a vertical ruler held in a clamp stand; a helical spring suspended from a G-clamp on a table edge with a mass hanging from it; a red dashed 'Equilibrium' line and a 'Displaced distance' marker; labels Ruler, Spring, Equilibrium, Displaced distance.

SVG: stopping-distance scene — a red car travelling at 20 m s⁻¹ on the left, three dashed marker posts labelled 'Hazard detected', 'Braking begins' and 'The car stops', a deer on the right; dimension brackets for 'Thinking distance 28 m', 'Braking distance 39 m' and an overall 'Stopping distance'; rolling green hills and clouds.

render_data.title: Classify the factors affecting stopping distance
render_data.prompt: Drag each factor into the correct category: environmental, driver, or car factor.

image_prompt: SVG: 2x3 grid of illustrated tiles each with a caption and a 'Select ▾' dropdown — Using a cell phone (driver looking at phone), Heavy rain, Worn out tyre tread (bald tyre), Faulty brakes (hot smoking brake), Drinking alcohol (wine glass and bottle), Poor road surface (cracked road).
Crop is question prose only (two paragraphs of driverless-cars text), no diagram to recreate.
